sanskrit: श्रीभगवानुवाच । अभयं सत्त्वसंशुद्धिर्ज्ञानयोगव्यवस्थितिः । दानं दमश्च यज्ञश्च स्वाध्यायस्तप आर्जवम् ॥
transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca | abhayaṃ sattva-saṃśuddhir jñāna-yoga-vyavasthitiḥ | dānaṃ damaś ca yajñaś ca svādhyāyas tapa ārjavam ||
literal_meaning: The Lord said: fearlessness, purity of being, steadiness in knowledge-yoga, giving, restraint of senses, sacrifice, self-study, austerity, straightforwardness.
contextual_meaning: Krishna lists the first cluster of divine traits—qualities that stabilize the mind for self-inquiry and ethical action—before contrasting them with demonic tendencies in later verses.
psychological_state: Optimal arousal for growth: low anxiety (abhaya), high internal organization (sattva-saṃśuddhi), and deliberate practice (jñāna-yoga-vyavasthitiḥ).
trigger_scenario: A client enters therapy saying, 'I want to change, but I keep slipping back into old habits.' This verse maps the first steps of therapeutic alliance: creating safety, clarifying values, and building consistent self-observation.
anchor_text: Live fearless, clear, steady, generous, restrained, studious, austere, and straight.
